discovery.run_summary: Risk-on tech tape driven by semis (SMH +2.6%, AVGO +5%, TSM +5.3%) on SK Hynix record memory results, while defense names lag (ITA -1.8%) despite Iran tanker interception headlines. Mid-cap dispersion is high: networking/comms names CALX and EXTR cratering likely on earnings, while Manhattan Associates gaps up. Overall tone is constructive but VIX +2.7% flags underlying Iran/geopolitical risk.

notable_index_moves: SMH +2.62% and TSM/AVGO leading on SK Hynix record HBM results
XLF -0.17% and KRE -0.55% lagging on the green tape - financials not participating
ITA -1.77% notable divergence given Iran tanker interception headlines; possible profit-taking after defense run
VIX +2.7% rising with SPY green is unusual - suggests hedging into Iran/earnings risk

discoveries.name: Calix, Inc
discoveries.mechanism: Likely earnings-driven gap; 4x volume and -14% with no peer weakness in software infrastructure suggests company-specific guide-down or margin miss
discoveries.catalyst: Not in supplied news feed - presumed Q1 report or guidance
research_pointers: Pull CALX earnings release and conference call transcript
Check BEAD program commentary - Calix is exposed to broadband infrastructure funding
Compare to EXTR (-5.8%) weakness same day - possible sector read on enterprise/service provider capex
Check guidance vs. consensus; Calix has history of lumpy revenue
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Evidence of a clean beat-and-raise with idiosyncratic technical selling, or conversely a genuine secular demand break (BEAD delays, tier-2/3 telco capex cuts)

discoveries.name: Extreme Networks
discoveries.mechanism: Possible sympathy selling with CALX on networking/enterprise capex concerns, or own earnings. 3x volume suggests information event not just sympathy
discoveries.catalyst: Unconfirmed - check earnings calendar and CALX read-through to enterprise networking
research_pointers: Confirm whether EXTR has its own catalyst or is trading sympathy to CALX
If pure sympathy, EXTR serves enterprise while CALX serves service providers - different end markets, potential overreaction
Check against peers ANET, JNPR, CSCO for relative weakness
discoveries.what_would_falsify: EXTR having its own negative pre-announcement; or peer networking names also down materially

discoveries.name: Manhattan Associates
discoveries.mechanism: Supply chain software name up on what looks like earnings; 5-day change +10.9% suggests momentum ahead of or following report
discoveries.catalyst: Likely Q1 earnings - not in feed
research_pointers: Verify earnings results and RPO/cloud subscription growth
Contrast with NOW (-14% yesterday on Iran war subscription hit) - MANH NOT showing same geopolitical drag is notable
Check if guidance raised
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Reversal if the move is short-covering without fundamental beat

discoveries.name: Repligen
discoveries.mechanism: Modest volume multiple (1.19x) for a -6% move suggests no blowout event - possibly earnings miss or bioprocessing demand commentary. XBI +0.67% so not sector driven
discoveries.catalyst: Unknown from provided feed - likely earnings
research_pointers: Check earnings release for bioprocessing orderbook and China commentary
Compare to peers DHR, SRT, AVTR for bioprocessing read-through
5-day -5.7% suggests some pre-positioning
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Clean beat with conservative guide (classic Repligen pattern); or genuine destocking extension that peers will confirm

discoveries.name: Best Buy
discoveries.mechanism: Down hard on a risk-on day with XLY flat. Low volume multiple suggests no single catalyst - possibly tariff refund headline read-through (Trump post re: $159B tariff refunds) negatively affecting consumer electronics pricing dynamics, or competitive pressure
discoveries.catalyst: No direct news; possibly tariff ruling aftermath affecting import-heavy consumer electronics retailers, or GME competitive bid speculation (GME +4.9%)
research_pointers: Check for analyst downgrade or pre-announcement
Review tariff Supreme Court decision impact on consumer electronics supply chain/pricing
Compare to other box retailers (COST, WMT) for relative read
Watch for read-through from AAPL +2.6% - if iPhone strong, BBY should participate
discoveries.what_would_falsify: A specific downgrade or competitive disruption would confirm; absence of catalyst with reversal would flag overdone

discoveries.name: Veeco Instruments
discoveries.mechanism: Sympathy with SMH +2.62% and SK Hynix record HBM results - Veeco supplies MOCVD/deposition equipment to memory/advanced packaging. 5-day +18% shows sustained re-rating
discoveries.catalyst: SK Hynix record profit on AI memory demand; broader semicap strength
research_pointers: Validate Veeco's HBM/advanced packaging revenue exposure
Check if move is pre-earnings positioning
Watch for read-through to other small-cap semicap (ONTO, KLIC, ACLS)
discoveries.what_would_falsify: Earnings that show no HBM uplift or China export restriction hit

catalyst_chains.primary_event: SK Hynix record Q1 profit on AI memory/HBM demand
ServiceNow -14% on Iran war subscription hit

secondary_opportunities.relationship: enterprise software peer - NOT showing same drag, potential relative winner
sympathy - networking peer, different end markets (enterprise vs. service provider)

trump_signals.post_summary: Trump attacking Supreme Court tariff ruling that ordered $159B in tariff refunds; suggests continued fight on tariff policy and unpredictable trade stance
trump_signals.note: Tariff refund ruling is a tailwind for import-heavy consumer names who paid tariffs, but Trump's hostile reaction raises uncertainty about future tariff regime. BBY weakness on a green tape while AAPL is +2.6% is notable - worth investigating whether tariff-refund benefit is being revisited or if there's BBY-specific news. Low conviction on direct causation.

ai_announcements.headline: Google updates Workspace with AI (Workspace Intelligence, Gmail AI Overviews, Chrome auto-browse, Gemini Enterprise Agent Platform)
ai_announcements.summary: Google rolled out a broad suite of AI features across Workspace, Chrome, Gmail and a new enterprise agent platform at Cloud Next 2026.

ai_announcements.headline: Google Cloud launches two new TPU AI chips to compete with Nvidia
ai_announcements.summary: New TPUs positioned as faster and cheaper than prior generation; Google still partnering with Nvidia in the near term.

affected_stocks.counter_thesis: More AI chip variants (TPU + Nvidia) means more semicap tool demand; Veeco's deposition/lithography tools benefit from broader advanced packaging/HBM buildout regardless of whose chip wins.
research_pointers: Confirm whether VECO move is tied to semicap tape (AI capex) vs. company-specific news/earnings
Check HBM/advanced packaging exposure of VECO relative to TPU ramp

ai_announcements.headline: OpenAI teams up with Infosys for enterprise AI deployment
ai_announcements.summary: Partnership focused on software engineering, legacy modernization, DevOps automation for Infosys clients.

ai_analysis.no_signals_note: Most of today's biggest movers don't map cleanly to the AI announcements. CALX -14% and EXTR -5.8% are networking/broadband names and more likely earnings/guidance-driven than AI-news driven (no direct AI headline targets them). MANH +5.9% and WEX +4.2% are not obvious AI casualties or beneficiaries from today's news. RGEN -6.25% is healthcare/bioprocessing, unrelated. BBY -4.6% and GME +4.9% are consumer retail, no clear AI-announcement linkage today. VECO's +5.3% is plausibly sympathy with Google TPU/AI capex narrative but could also be semicap-wide tape. Quiet night for AI-driven mispricings in this tape; recommend not forcing signals.
